flashcards.back: A matrix where each element is either 0 or 1. Used to represent relationships between two sets, particularly in graph theory for representing adjacency and incidence.

flashcards.front: Define Adjacency Matrix of a Directed Graph
flashcards.back: A square matrix A where A[i][j] = 1 if there is a directed edge from vertex i to vertex j, and 0 otherwise. For n vertices, it's an n×n matrix.
A graph where edges have a specific direction, from a starting vertex (tail) to an ending vertex (head). Represented as G = (V, E) where E is a set of ordered pairs.
A matrix B where rows represent vertices and columns represent edges. B[i][j] = 1 if vertex i is incident to edge j, 0 otherwise. For digraphs: -1 for tail, 1 for head, 0 otherwise.
The transitive closure of a digraph G is a graph G* containing all vertices where an edge exists between vertices if and only if there is a path from one to the other in the original graph.
Warshall's Algorithm computes the transitive closure of a digraph using dynamic programming. It efficiently finds all reachable vertices from any starting vertex through matrix operations.
A matrix P where P[i][j] = 1 if there exists at least one path from vertex i to vertex j. It's essentially the transitive closure of the adjacency matrix.
A directed graph is strongly connected if there is a directed path from every vertex to every other vertex. Each vertex can reach all other vertices.
A directed graph is weakly connected if replacing all its directed edges with undirected edges produces a connected (undirected) graph.
Matrix multiplication using Boolean algebra where AND replaces multiplication and OR replaces addition. (A∧B) means both conditions true, (A∨B) means at least one true.
Outdegree of a vertex = number of edges leaving it. Indegree = number of edges entering it. In adjacency matrix: row sum = outdegree, column sum = indegree.
A matrix R where R[i][j] = 1 if vertex j is reachable from vertex i through some path (including direct edge). It represents all possible connections in the graph.

flashcards.front: What is Computer Recognition in graph context?
flashcards.back: The process of using matrix representations to enable computers to identify, store, analyze, and process graph-structured data efficiently using zero-one matrices.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of Warshall's Algorithm?
flashcards.back: O(n³) where n is the number of vertices. This is because it uses three nested loops to compute the transitive closure.

flashcards.front: How are Zero-One matrices used in computer graphics?
flashcards.back: Zero-one matrices represent pixel patterns, image masks, and binary features. They enable efficient pattern recognition, image processing, and morphological operations in computer vision.
